key_insight: Variable-area superpixels can compress large 2D diffraction datasets while preserving high-information regions and symmetry, making downstream analysis more interpretable.
engineering_relevance: This preprocessing step reduces the computational burden of 4D-STEM and synchrotron diffraction pipelines, enabling much faster phase mapping and scalable AI-driven materials analysis.
method: Group pixels into superpixels of different sizes based on local variance/information content.
Compare top-down K-means clustering with bottom-up agglomerative clustering for superpixel construction.
Accelerate distance-based superpixel formation with Gaussian random projection before clustering.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports superior scaling and interpretability for agglomerative clustering and more than 100-fold acceleration for non-negative matrix factorization phase mapping on a 4D-STEM dataset.
watch_item: Because the approach is distance-based and data-dependent, results may be sensitive to clustering choices, projection settings, and the degree to which important local features are preserved.
paper_title: Using superpixels for interpretable feature reduction in large 2D diffraction datasets
